form.label: Anthropic Compliance Overview
form.description: Security analyst view of Anthropic Compliance API data

input.label: User Filter (email or ID, * for all)

search.query: `anthropic_compliance_index` sourcetype="anthropic:compliance:activity" actor.type=user_actor | eval user=coalesce('actor.email_address','actor.user_id') | where like(user, replace("$user_filter$", "\*", "%")) | timechart count by type span=1h limit=10

`anthropic_compliance_index` sourcetype="anthropic:compliance:activity" actor.type=user_actor | eval user=coalesce('actor.email_address','actor.user_id') | where like(user, replace("$user_filter$", "\*", "%")) | stats count as events dc(type) as distinct_actions latest(_time) as last_seen by user | sort -events | head 20 | eval last_seen=strftime(last_seen, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S") | rename user as "User", events as "Events", distinct_actions as "Action Types", last_seen as "Last Seen"

`anthropic_compliance_index` sourcetype="anthropic:compliance:activity" (match(type, "created") OR match(type, "updated") OR match(type, "deleted") OR match(type, "removed") OR match(type, "added") OR match(type, "invited")) | eval user=coalesce('actor.email_address','actor.unauthenticated_email_address','actor.user_id','actor.api_key_id','actor.admin_api_key_id') | where like(user, replace("$user_filter$", "\*", "%")) | timechart count by type span=1h limit=10

`anthropic_compliance_index` sourcetype="anthropic:compliance:org_user" | eval user=coalesce(email, id) | where like(user, replace("$user_filter$", "\*", "%")) | dedup user sortby -_time | table user role _org_name created_at | sort user | rename user as "User", role as "Role", _org_name as "Org", created_at as "Member Since"

`anthropic_compliance_index` sourcetype="anthropic:compliance:group_member" | eval user=coalesce(email, user_id) | where like(user, replace("$user_filter$", "\*", "%")) | dedup user _group_id sortby -_time | stats values(_group_name) as groups count as memberships by user | sort -memberships | rename user as "User", groups as "Groups", memberships as "Memberships"
